description: Enterprise techniques used by Fooder, ATT&CK software S9033 (v1.0)
name: Fooder (S9033)

techniques.comment: [Fooder](https://attack.mitre.org/software/S9033) has used the `DuplicateTokenEx` API to duplicate the token of a specified process, and `CreateProcessAsUserA` to execute its payload.(Citation: ESET_MuddyWater_Dec2025)

[Fooder](https://attack.mitre.org/software/S9033) has used a custom delay function (`delayExecution(integer)`) and Sleep API calls (`Sleep(integer)`) to slow code execution.(Citation: ESET_MuddyWater_Dec2025)
[Fooder](https://attack.mitre.org/software/S9033) has decrypted payloads using the WinCrypt API and the AES key.(Citation: ESET_MuddyWater_Dec2025)

[Fooder](https://attack.mitre.org/software/S9033) has frequently masqueraded as the Snake game, using strings such as “Welcome to snake Game” and mutexes such as “SNAKE_G.”(Citation: ESET_MuddyWater_Dec2025)

[Fooder](https://attack.mitre.org/software/S9033) has used the WinCrypt API for payload decryption, `DuplicateTokenEx` to duplicate the token of a specified process, and `CreateProcessAsUserA` for payload execution.(Citation: ESET_MuddyWater_Dec2025)

[Fooder](https://attack.mitre.org/software/S9033) has stored its embedded payload in encrypted form within the binary, using a hardcoded key modified at runtime to produce the AES decryption key.(Citation: ESET_MuddyWater_Dec2025)
[Fooder](https://attack.mitre.org/software/S9033) has reflectively loaded a payload into memory.(Citation: ESET_MuddyWater_Dec2025)
